description: Study 8 corpus sampling-fragility MARGIN sweep (CPU-only). For each of the 5 continuous-domain geometric problems, sweep a naive uniform textual-verifier's resolution N=3..400 across 12 grid alignments; record N_any (>=1 alignment catches the falsifier) and N_all (every alignment catches). geom_05 (sequence) and geom_09 (diff-quotient) probed separately as heuristic-specific.
motivation: verify.py asserts 'sampling-fragile' as a BINARY property at one hand-picked naive grid (N=5-11). A skeptic can object the miss is cherry-picked to that resolution/alignment, undermining H8's premise that numeric sampling cannot easily win. Quantify how robust the fragility actually is.
formal_results: Robust-catch threshold N_all (designer naive grid in parens): geom_01 22 (11), geom_03 37 (11), geom_04 20 (5x5), geom_06 86 (11), geom_10 17 (11). N_any as low as 3-10 for all. geom_05: dense integer scan catches at prefix K=45 (sparse logspace {1,2,5,10,20,50,100,200} misses). geom_09: decimal-h heuristic gives max|sin(pi/h)|=4.1e-12 (ratifies false f'(0)=0) but ANY generic uniform h-grid catches at N=4.
results_description: Every continuous problem is robustly caught by a moderately denser UNIFORM sampler at N_all=17-86; the two non-continuous problems are caught trivially by a dense integer scan (K=45) or any generic h-grid (N=4). The designer's naive grids (N=5-11) miss only because they are coarse/heuristic, not because sampling is in-principle blind to the falsifier.
conclusions: The corpus's sampling-fragility is REAL but SHALLOW and partly heuristic-specific: a forced_textual verifier allowed N~100 uniform points (or a dense integer scan) would catch every falsifier. So H8's premise ('numeric sampling cannot easily win') holds only relative to the LOW-resolution/heuristic sampler the agent tends to use, not as an in-principle barrier. METHODOLOGICAL COROLLARY for Study 8: a forced_visual 'win' would hinge on the forced_textual baseline's sampler being crippled (low N / sparse / decimal-h); to be a fair test of visual advantage, forced_textual must be pinned to a documented low-resolution strategy and that choice pre-registered, else any visual edge is a baseline artifact.
